title: Simplify root AGENTS.md to Bootstrap Protocol + werewolf fixture Shadow Mirroring sync
description: Simplify the root AGENTS.md template to contain only the Fabric Bootstrap Protocol (no @import lines, no submodule rules, no Scope Map / Hard Rules / Semantic Roster content). Rewrite examples/werewolf-minigame-stub/AGENTS.md as a Shadow Mirroring example — it should demonstrate that the project's AI constraints live in .fabric/agents/ not in AGENTS.md itself. Create the corresponding .fabric/agents/ mirror structure for the werewolf example (root.md + individual agent files mirroring assets/scripts/). This makes the werewolf example a living reference for Shadow Mirroring topology and shows users what the SKILL (TASK-002) will generate.

files.target: Entire file — Scope Map, Hard Rules, Semantic Roster, Agent sections
files.change: Rewrite to Bootstrap Protocol only: (1) header identifying this as Fabric Bootstrap Protocol, (2) reference to .fabric/agents/ as the location of all AI constraints, (3) brief instruction to run fab_get_rules before any code reading/planning/editing, (4) pointer to .fabric/agents/root.md for full constraint set. Remove all Scope Map, Hard Rules, Semantic Roster, Agent sections, Collaboration Model, Cross-Agent Safety, @HUMAN Lock Range Example — these migrate to .fabric/agents/ mirror structure.

files.target: New file — L0 root shadow constraints
files.change: Create L0 root.md containing: Repository Identity (project type, fixture purpose), Scope Map (protected surfaces: assets/prefabs/**, assets/scenes/**, **/*.meta), Hard Rules MUST/NEVER (same content as current AGENTS.md Hard Rules section), Working Contract, Cross-Agent Safety Rules, Drift Expectations, Review Checklist. This becomes the Shadow Mirroring L0 equivalent of the current colocated AGENTS.md content.

files.target: New file — L1 Villager agent shadow rules
files.change: Create mirror file for villager agent: Mission, Owned Decisions, Visible Information, Collaboration Contract, MUST/NEVER rules from current AGENTS.md Agent: Villager section. Path mirrors assets/scripts/villager.ts (or equivalent).

files.target: New file — L1 Werewolf agent shadow rules
files.change: Create mirror file for werewolf agent from current AGENTS.md Agent: Werewolf section.

files.target: New file — cross-cutting role balance constraints
files.change: Create cross-cutting rules file for cross-agent safety: role-balance edit requires human review, resolution order changes require written explanation, private-information flow changes require maintainer sign-off. Includes @HUMAN Lock Range Example. scope_glob: '**' (global cross-cutting).

implementation: Read examples/werewolf-minigame-stub/AGENTS.md in full before modifying
Identify which sections migrate to root.md (Repository Identity, Scope Map, Hard Rules, Working Contract), which to agent files (Agent: X sections), which to _cross/ (Cross-Agent Safety, @HUMAN Lock)
Create .fabric/agents/ directory structure in werewolf fixture
Write .fabric/agents/root.md with L0 content (migrated from AGENTS.md global sections)
Rewrite examples/werewolf-minigame-stub/AGENTS.md as Bootstrap Protocol only (≤ 30 lines)
Verify: no game-specific content remains in AGENTS.md root; all semantic content in .fabric/agents/

reference.pattern: Shadow Mirroring topology: .fabric/agents/ 1:1 source mirror + _cross/ subtree for cross-cutting
reference.examples: conclusions.json round3_delta.new_technical_solutions[0] defines Shadow Mirroring: .fabric/agents/ 1:1 source mirror tree; business dirs (src/) contain ZERO rule files

criteria: examples/werewolf-minigame-stub/AGENTS.md ≤ 30 lines, contains no Scope Map / Hard Rules / Agent sections
examples/werewolf-minigame-stub/.fabric/agents/root.md exists and contains Repository Identity + Scope Map + global Hard Rules
grep -r 'SCOPE MAP' examples/werewolf-minigame-stub/AGENTS.md returns zero matches (migrated to .fabric/agents/)
The .fabric/agents/ directory structure mirrors the source directory structure (assets/scripts/ → .fabric/agents/assets/scripts/)

rationale.chosen_approach: Migrate content from colocated AGENTS.md to .fabric/agents/ mirror tree, leaving AGENTS.md as Bootstrap Protocol only — makes the werewolf fixture a living example of Shadow Mirroring topology that users can reference
alternatives_considered: Keep AGENTS.md as-is and only add .fabric/agents/ alongside it (creates confusion about which is authoritative during migration)
decision_factors: R3-CQ2: product requires MCP-capable clients; AGENTS.md is not read by MCP; .fabric/agents/ is
werewolf example is the primary reference fixture for Fabric documentation and tests
Bootstrap Protocol only in AGENTS.md is consistent with TASK-005 bootstrap template upgrade
rationale.tradeoffs: Werewolf fixture content is reorganized, not reduced — all semantic content preserved in .fabric/agents/; test fixtures referencing AGENTS.md sections may need updates

manual_checks: ls examples/werewolf-minigame-stub/.fabric/agents/ — should show root.md, assets/, _cross/ directories
success_metrics: AGENTS.md root is Bootstrap Protocol only (verified by line count and grep for removed sections)
.fabric/agents/ mirror tree contains all migrated semantic content
No game-specific content lost (grep across .fabric/agents/ finds all original Agent sections)
integration: TASK-007 e2e fixture test: sync-meta run on werewolf fixture produces agents.meta.json with entries for all .fabric/agents/ files

risks.description: Existing tests or documentation may reference specific line numbers or sections of werewolf AGENTS.md
risks.mitigation: Preserve all semantic content in .fabric/agents/; update any tests that reference specific AGENTS.md sections
risks.fallback: Add redirect comment in AGENTS.md pointing to the new .fabric/agents/ location
